svg.text: CASE STUDY — MONEY LAUNDERING VIA DECENTRALIZED MIXER
Case AML-IR-2026-004 · Tornado Cash · Lazarus Group (DPRK) · USD 7.6B processed · Verdict: August 2025
Financial Intelligence Alert Indicators — AML Red Flags

g.text: • Complete absence of VASP, KYC, and STR — AML framework has no entry point
• Fixed denomination deposits — structuring via pool selection (ACAMS 3.1.4)
• On-chain link deposit/withdrawal broken — tracing computationally infeasible
• Relayer use: withdrawal from wallet with zero ETH balance (ACAMS 5.1.1)
• State-sponsored sanctioned actor — DPRK Lazarus Group (SDN since 2019)
• Immutable code: impossible to block, freeze, or disable
• Criminal proceeds: DeFi / cross-chain bridge hacks (predicate offence confirmed)
Frameworks: ACAMS 4.3.3 · FATF R.16 · FATF VA Guidance §5.2/5.3 · IEEPA · BSA · EU 6AMLD Art.2
Integration phase remains the only effective enforcement point — this is where investigative focus applies

svg.text: Central Legal Question — No Definitive Answer
"Can a developer be criminally liable for how third parties use code they no longer control?"

g.text: • Netherlands (Pertsev): YES — convicted 64 months. TC lacked anti-abuse measures.
• USA — jury (Storm): UNDECIDED — guilty of unlicensed MTB, deadlocked on laundering and sanctions.
• USA — 5th Circuit: NO (sanctions) — immutable code ≠ "property" under IEEPA. OFAC exceeded authority.
• DOJ (Apr 2025): acknowledged "regulation by prosecution" — criminal process is not a regulatory tool.
• TC continues to run today. Immutable. Semenov at large. Question remains open.
AML takeaway: Integration phase is the only real enforcement point. Decentralized mixers break everything else.
Frameworks: ACAMS CAMS 10th Ed. · FATF R.15 §5 · 18 U.S.C. §1960 · IEEPA · BSA/FinCEN · EU 6AMLD

svg.text: Author: Phelipe Agnelli — AML & Blockchain Forensics · Sources: OFAC Treasury.gov · DOJ SDNY · 5th Circuit Van Loon v. Treasury · Dutch FIOD · Chainalysis · ACAMS · FATF
